steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content:
The word even signals a surprising consequence: even the once-revolutionary dual-core technology is now commonplace, which proves how drastically processor power has increased.

This relationship—a main claim followed by an illustrative consequence—determines the punctuation between the two parts.

steps.title: Match the punctuation to the grammar
steps.content:
A dash correctly introduces a dependent clause that elaborates on or illustrates the main clause.

The subordinating conjunction that marks the second part as subordinate, not a separate sentence.

Option A creates a comma splice with no coordinating conjunction joining the two independent clauses.

Option B has malformed punctuation. Option D uses a period, which wrongly separates the elaboration from the main thought and leaves “That the once-revolutionary...” as an incomplete sentence without a predicate.

The dash-plus-subordinating-conjunction construction in C captures the precise relationship: cores are so numerous that the once-revolutionary technology is now in everyday items.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
